Selected Works

Games I've Built

Horror, diplomacy, VR rhythm, and narrative mystery — four genres, four different technical challenges.

ShippedGame DesignProgrammingArt Direction

Big Brother

A retro horror adventure where a watchful eye hunts you through pixelated corridors. Outsmart its patrol routes, dodge its traps, and escape — or be caught.

  • AI with Patrol → Chase → Search state machine and NavMesh pathfinding
  • Custom retro PS1 rendering pipeline: low-res Render Texture + 4:3 aspect ratio + VHS post-processing
  • Procedural multi-leg walk animation using sine-wave coordination
Case Study →Trailer ↗
ShippedGame DesignProgrammingArt

Roll for Resolution

A sci-fi strategy game where you play as an alien Mediator racing to save Earth from an apocalyptic threat. Negotiate with divided global powers, draft tactical cards, manage the Doom Clock, and roll dice to overcome unique national traits and secure world peace.

  • 10+ card types across 4 categories: Basic Stats, Dice Manipulation, Risk & Special, Universal Mechanics
  • National Trait system with mathematical formulas affecting gameplay (Strict, PanicProne, FixedDie, Elitist)
  • Doom Clock — failed negotiations advance it; game ends when the hour hand completes a full rotation
Case Study →Trailer ↗
PrototypeGame DesignVR ProgrammingInteraction Design

F1 Pit Stop VR

A rhythm-based VR pit stop simulation. Synchronize your wheel gun maneuvers with the beat to secure bolts with mechanical precision — blending professional racing maintenance with addictive rhythmic flow.

  • Rhythm-based VR interaction: synchronize actions (unscrew, pull, push) with timed beats
  • Bilateral validation — both hands must act as a unified input source to trigger actions
  • Temporal thresholding — filters out high-frequency noise, requiring 50ms sustained velocity
Case Study →Trailer ↗
JamGame DevelopmentProgramming

What If…?

Regretting a bitter argument, you are thrust back in time to your mother's doomed, burning subway car. Manage limited Action Points to investigate suspicious passengers and wield unconventional weapons. Unmask the arsonist and change fate in this emotional adventure before time runs out.

  • Global Game Jam project — first time collaborating with artists and musicians
  • Branching dialogue system with 3 distinct endings driven by Action Point economy
  • First time integrating FMOD for adaptive audio in Unity
Case Study →Trailer ↗
View All Games →

About

Systems thinker. Aesthetic committer.

I'm Annie — a game designer and developer who treats every project as a conversation between art and engineering. I like games where the visual identity and the mechanics speak the same language.

From PS1-era horror AI to satirical geopolitics-as-dice, I build with range and intention.
